evals.prompt: Use $triage on the current branch and tell me if it can land.
expectations: Resolves the PR from the current branch without creating or editing a PR.
Reads draft state, merge state, review decision, checks, and head/base.
Returns one readiness status with blockers and evidence.
Names the smallest next command without mutating local or remote state.

evals.prompt: This PR has failing checks and requested changes. Use $triage to tell me what is blocking it.
expectations: Classifies the PR as needing repair instead of ready to land.
Separates failing checks from review-request blockers.
Does not patch code, comment, resolve threads, or merge.
Recommends $repair-pr for actionable same-PR failures or comments.

evals.prompt: Use $triage for PR #42 and include whether unresolved conversations may block merge.
expectations: Uses PR metadata and GraphQL thread state when conversation resolution matters.
Marks thread state unknown if GitHub does not expose enough information.
Does not resolve conversations.
Explains whether conversation state is a known blocker or an uncertainty.

evals.prompt: Use $triage to check whether the PR diff still matches the base branch before landing.
expectations: Compares PR files and local merge-base diff when scope or base matters.
Reports any head/base ambiguity.
Keeps the workflow read-only apart from metadata fetches.
Routes ready PRs to $land-pr and scope problems to a user decision or $ship.

evals.prompt: Use $triage to review the open PR queue and tell me what needs attention.
expectations: Lists open PRs without mutating files, branches, comments, labels, or merge state.
Groups PRs by action such as ready to land, needs repair, needs review, pending, blocked, or unknown.
Uses plain language instead of raw GitHub enums or command output.
Recommends the smallest next command for each actionable group.
